title: poa glauca subsp. conferta
intro: Poa glauca subsp. conferta, also known as blue fescue or tussock grass, is a subspecies of grass that is native to New Zealand and Australia. It is a type of ornamental grass that is valued for its blue-green foliage and ability to thrive in poor soil conditions. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, and has been shown to have potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving digestion. Poa glauca subsp. conferta can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, or infusions, and is often used in combination with other herbs to support overall health and well-being.
